evals.prompt: Our team just shipped a component library redesign and now 23 tests are failing with TimeoutError on broken locators. Walk me through the process to bulk-regenerate selectors.
expected_patterns: Phase 1 — Snapshot the old DOM OR old-dom snapshot
Phase 2 — Snapshot the new DOM OR new-dom snapshot
Phase 3 — Identify broken selectors OR capture failures
Phase 4 — Generate replacement candidates OR role-first
Phase 5 — Validate OR run suite
Phase 6 — Ship the PR OR per-change evidence
confidence score OR stability rubric

evals.prompt: I have a Playwright JSON report showing 15 failed tests after a Tailwind CSS upgrade. Extract the broken locators, their line numbers, and what they're trying to interact with.
expected_patterns: JSON report parsing OR locator extraction
TimeoutError OR locator.* exceeded
line number OR source location
inferred intent OR what assertion follows
table OR structured output
test file grouping

evals.prompt: I need to snapshot the old DOM before our redesign. We have a green CI artifact with Playwright traces from the last successful run. How do I extract and save the HTML snapshots?
expected_patterns: CI artifact OR trace extraction
npx playwright show-trace OR trace files
page.content() OR innerHTML
.drift-recovery/old OR snapshot directory
per-page snapshots OR per-component
anti_patterns: no reference to CI artifacts OR baseline

evals.prompt: The old selector was 'locator(".order-summary > h2")' with intent 'verify order total visible'. Generate replacement candidates with confidence scores for the new DOM.
expected_patterns: getByRole OR getByLabel OR getByTestId
data-testid OR score 5
accessible name OR score 4
region-scoped OR neighbor context OR score 3
visible text only OR score 2
CSS class OR score 1
no replacement OR score 0 OR human review
confidence score output
anti_patterns: xpath OR css= selectors in replacement

evals.prompt: I've applied 47 selector replacements to a feature branch. Now I need to validate the full test suite before merging. What should I do if some tests pass and some fail?
expected_patterns: run affected test suite OR full suite
revert failing replacements OR mark for review
keep passing replacements
generate summary OR N recovered, M flagged
clear marking of flagged tests
all recovered tests pass

evals.prompt: Create a PR description for a selector recovery after a redesign. Include summary counts, per-file change tables with confidence scores, and guidance for the reviewer.
expected_patterns: Trigger OR Link to refactor PR
Summary OR N test files, M selectors changed
Per-file changes OR table with line, old, new, score
Flagged for review OR manual review section
How to review OR screenshot verification
confidence score OR screenshot URL
chore(tests) OR PR title
anti_patterns: all changes in one block without grouping
flagged tests mixed with recovered ones

evals.prompt: After recovering selectors, our average selector stability score dropped from 3.7 to 3.2. What went wrong?
expected_patterns: auto-applied score-2 candidates OR over-confidence
not preferring getByRole OR role-first
CSS class-based replacements OR brittle selectors
not ratcheting up stability after recovery
recovered tests should have higher score
update or revert low-scoring candidates

evals.prompt: Our designer removed the product carousel entirely in the redesign. Should we regenerate the selector for the carousel 'next' button, or handle this differently?
expected_patterns: delete test OR mark for deletion
removed feature OR feature no longer exists
prune tests OR tests no longer make sense
Phase 6 OR deleted tests section
not regenerating selectors for missing elements

evals.prompt: I have 200 broken selectors across 50 test files. Is selector-drift-recovery the right skill?
expected_patterns: bulk update OR many selectors
event-driven OR refactor shipped OR redesign
not single flaky test OR test-reliability
split by area OR split PR OR review scope
too large to review in one sitting OR gate

evals.prompt: My team says we should auto-merge the selector recovery PR in CI once all tests pass. Is that appropriate?
expected_patterns: no auto-merge OR human reviewer required
PR is the artifact OR review is essential
reviewer eyeballs per-change evidence
screenshots must be verified
confidence scores need signoff
anti-pattern OR not recommended
